question_template: Considering the recording environment of this clip, is the reverberation time RT60 at least {threshold} seconds? Answer yes or no.
answer_template: {'yes' if RT60 >= threshold else 'no'}

question_template: For this recording environment, is the measured reverberation time RT60 greater than {threshold} seconds?
answer_template: {'yes' if RT60 > threshold else 'no'}

question_template: Based on this audio clip, is the reverberation time RT60 at most {threshold} seconds? Answer yes or no.
answer_template: {'yes' if RT60 <= threshold else 'no'}

answer_template: {'yes' if RT60 < threshold else 'no'}

question_template: Does the measured RT60 for this recording fall in the interval [{lower}, {upper}) seconds? Answer yes or no.
answer_template: {'yes' if lower <= RT60 < upper else 'no'}

question_template: For the recording environment captured in this clip, is the reverberation time RT60 between {lower} seconds and {upper} seconds, inclusive of the lower bound and exclusive of the upper bound?

question_template: Is the reverberation time RT60 measured from this recording inside the predefined band from {lower} s to {upper} s?

question_template: Does the reverberation time RT60 of this clip belong to the range [{lower}, {upper}) seconds?

question_template: Using the measured reverberation time RT60 of this recording, classify the recording environment with this rule: very dry if RT60 < 0.3, small room if 0.3 <= RT60 < 0.6, medium hall if 0.6 <= RT60 < 1.2, large hall if 1.2 <= RT60 < 2.5, highly reverberant if RT60 >= 2.5.
answer_template: {'very dry' if RT60 < 0.3 else 'small room' if RT60 < 0.6 else 'medium hall' if RT60 < 1.2 else 'large hall' if RT60 < 2.5 else 'highly reverberant'}

question_template: For this clip, use the RT60 of the recording environment to choose a category under this rule: (A) RT60 < 0.3, (B) 0.3 <= RT60 < 0.6, (C) 0.6 <= RT60 < 1.2, (D) 1.2 <= RT60 < 2.5, (E) RT60 >= 2.5.
answer_template: {'(A)' if RT60 < 0.3 else '(B)' if RT60 < 0.6 else '(C)' if RT60 < 1.2 else '(D)' if RT60 < 2.5 else '(E)'}

question_template: Using the RT60 measured for this recording, choose the matching environment label: anechoic or very dry room if RT60 < 0.3, small practice room or home studio if 0.3 <= RT60 < 0.6, medium-sized recital hall or recording studio if 0.6 <= RT60 < 1.2, large concert hall or theater if 1.2 <= RT60 < 2.5, cathedral or highly reverberant venue if RT60 >= 2.5.
answer_template: {'anechoic or very dry room' if RT60 < 0.3 else 'small practice room or home studio' if RT60 < 0.6 else 'medium-sized recital hall or recording studio' if RT60 < 1.2 else 'large concert hall or theater' if RT60 < 2.5 else 'cathedral or highly reverberant venue'}

question_template: For the recording environment in this sample, use the RT60 to decide between these two labels: room-like or drier if RT60 < 1.2, and hall-like or more reverberant if RT60 >= 1.2.

question_template: For this recording environment, is the reverberation time RT60 above, below, or exactly at the midpoint of the full RT60 range from 0.053 to 8.808 seconds?

question_template: For this recording, is the measured RT60 closer to 0.3 seconds or 0.6 seconds?

question_template: Please listen to the sample. For the recording environment here, apply this rule to the measured RT60: studio-like if RT60 < 0.6, performance-space-like if 0.6 <= RT60 < 2.5, cathedral-like if RT60 >= 2.5.
answer_template: {'studio-like' if RT60 < 0.6 else 'performance-space-like' if RT60 < 2.5 else 'cathedral-like'}

question_template: Listen to the recording and use the RT60 measured from it to choose one label under this rule: low if RT60 < 0.3, mid-low if 0.3 <= RT60 < 0.6, mid if 0.6 <= RT60 < 1.2, mid-high if 1.2 <= RT60 < 2.5, high if RT60 >= 2.5.
answer_template: {'low' if RT60 < 0.3 else 'mid-low' if RT60 < 0.6 else 'mid' if RT60 < 1.2 else 'mid-high' if RT60 < 2.5 else 'high'}

question_template: After listening to this clip, pick the matching band from its reverberation time RT60: Band 1 for RT60 < 0.3, Band 2 for 0.3 <= RT60 < 0.6, Band 3 for 0.6 <= RT60 < 1.2, Band 4 for 1.2 <= RT60 < 2.5, Band 5 for RT60 >= 2.5.
answer_template: {'Band 1' if RT60 < 0.3 else 'Band 2' if RT60 < 0.6 else 'Band 3' if RT60 < 1.2 else 'Band 4' if RT60 < 2.5 else 'Band 5'}

question_template: Listen to the audio. Categorize the reverberation time RT60 of this recording environment with this rule: near-anechoic if RT60 < 0.3, domestic-room range if 0.3 <= RT60 < 0.6, medium-space range if 0.6 <= RT60 < 1.2, large-space range if 1.2 <= RT60 < 2.5, monumental-space range if RT60 >= 2.5.
answer_template: {'near-anechoic' if RT60 < 0.3 else 'domestic-room range' if RT60 < 0.6 else 'medium-space range' if RT60 < 1.2 else 'large-space range' if RT60 < 2.5 else 'monumental-space range'}

question_template: Please listen to this sample. Using its measured RT60, decide whether the recording environment is closer to dry-space behavior (RT60 < 0.6), hall behavior (0.6 <= RT60 < 2.5), or extreme-reverb behavior (RT60 >= 2.5).
answer_template: {'dry-space behavior' if RT60 < 0.6 else 'hall behavior' if RT60 < 2.5 else 'extreme-reverb behavior'}

question_template: After listening to this clip, map the RT60 to one of these descriptive bins: crisp if RT60 < 0.3, lightly live if 0.3 <= RT60 < 0.6, live if 0.6 <= RT60 < 1.2, very live if 1.2 <= RT60 < 2.5, overwhelmingly live if RT60 >= 2.5.
answer_template: {'crisp' if RT60 < 0.3 else 'lightly live' if RT60 < 0.6 else 'live' if RT60 < 1.2 else 'very live' if RT60 < 2.5 else 'overwhelmingly live'}

question_template: Listen to this clip. If the desired RT60 is from {low} to {high} seconds, should the recording environment become more reverberant, drier, or stay unchanged?
answer_template: {'more reverberant' if RT60 < low else 'drier' if RT60 >= high else 'stay unchanged'}
